info.description:
Customer Relationship Management Service

 **Last update**: 22 dic 25, 10:30 a. m.
info.title: CRM Service

tags.name: Cluster
tags.description:
Clusters are a way to group customers by some condition, for example: recurring customers, customers in some affinity program, etc.

You can configure clusters in order to show a badge in the customer to easily identify them.

tags.name: Customer
tags.description:
Real customers are created and updated automatically when they place an order. But in case you want to add more customers manually, o update them with external sources of information,
you can use the following APIs

Customers are identified uniquely so we can recognize, for example, a customer that uses multiple email addresses. That way you can effectively manage them and have better customer relationships.

requestBody.description:
### Customer identification

It must have at least one of the following unique identifiers.
- `documentType` and `documentNumber`
- `email`

### Customer emails and phones

The following fields can hold multiple values:
- `phone` and `phones`
- `email` and `emails`
- `clusters`

When updating a customer, you can send the main value in the single-value field (for example `email`),
and alternative values (such as an alternative email) in the multi-valued field (for example `emails`)

Whether or not you send alternative values, the multi-valued fields will store all the possible values. For example, if you send the following request:
```json
{
  "email": "john.doe@example.com",
  "emails": ["john.doe@alternative.com"]
}
```

We will store `"john.doe@example.com"` as the primary `email`, and both addresses in the customers `emails` property.

This works the same way with the customer phone numbers.

Also, there are a couple of special fields you can use to add alternative values, without needing to know current values.

This properties are: `addEmails`, `addPhones` and `addClusters`. This properties will add one or more values to the multi-valued fields without overriding the existing ones.

---

### Customer identification

It must have at least one of the following unique identifiers.
- `documentType` and `documentNumber`
- `email`

### Customer emails and phones

The following fields can hold multiple values:
- `phone` and `phones`
- `email` and `emails`

When creating a customer, you can send the main value in the single-value field (for example `email`),
and alternative values (such as an alternative email) in the multi-valued field (for example `emails`)

Whether or not you send alternative values, the multi-valued fields will store all the possible values. For example, if you send the following request:
```json
{
  "email": "john.doe@example.com",
  "emails": ["john.doe@alternative.com"]
}
```

We will store `"john.doe@example.com"` as the primary `email`, and both addresses in the customers `emails` property.

This works the same way with the customer phone numbers.

---

CustomerAddressBatchResponse.description: Response object for batch creation or update of customer addresses. Includes lists of created, updated, unchanged, and errored addresses.

itemsCreated.description: List of customer addresses successfully created

itemsUpdated.description: List of customer addresses successfully updated

itemsNotModified.description: List of customer addresses that were not modified (no changes detected)

itemsError.description: List of customer addresses that encountered errors during processing

id.description: The unique identifier of the item

userCreated.description: The ID of the user who created the item

userModified.description: The ID of the user who last modified the item

dateModified.description: The date of the last modification

Cluster.description: A cluster of customers. Clusters can be used to group clients, show badges for them and segment your customer DB

CustomerWithDocumentIdentifier.description: A customer unique identifier based on it's document

CustomerWithEmailIdentifier.description: A customer unique identifier based on it's email

firstname.description: The customer firstname. It's only required to create the customer, not to update it.

lastname.description: The customer lastname. It's only required to create the customer, not to update it.

emails.description: A list with all the customer emails

phones.description: A list with all the customer phones

clusters.description: The list with all the customer cluster IDs

habeasData.description: If the customer accepted the habeas data and the last modified

accepted.description: If the customer accepted the habeas data

CustomerAppend.description: Customer fields to allow appending values to lists

addEmails.description: Emails to append to the existing ones

addPhones.description: Phones to append to the existing ones

addClusters.description: Cluster IDs to append to the existing ones

geoVerified.description:
Indicates if the address geolocation (`lat` and `lng`) has been verified. This can happen in two ways:

1. Automatically: When three consecutive deliveries made through Janis Delivery APP are in a 100-meter area.
2. Manually: By setting this field to `true`

If a verified address is modified (for example, the street number changes), the automatic geo verification will reset:
- `oldCoordinates` and `coordinatesDeliveries` are removed
- `geoVerified` is set to `false`

oldCoordinates.description: This is set to the original coordinates (if any) when an address geolocation is automatically verified

coordinatesDeliveries.description:
Stores the coordinates of the last 3 deliveries made in for this address with the Janis Delivery APP.

This is used for the automatic geo verification (see `geoVerified`)

items.description: The coordinate of a delivery made for this address with the Janis Delivery APP

needsAddressCompletion.description:
Indicates if the address needs to be completed. 

- This is set to `true` when the address is incomplete (when the `state` is empty or the `city` and `neighborhood` are empty)
- This is set to `false` when the address is complete.

CustomerAddressBatchBase.description: Base object containing shared attributes required for creating or updating a customer address

geoVerified.description: Whether the address is geo verified

CustomerAddressToCreate.title: Customer address to create
CustomerAddressToCreate.description:
Object representing the required data to create a new customer address.
    
Including customer ID, address details, and optional geo verification status

CustomerAddressToUpdate.title: Customer address to update
CustomerAddressToUpdate.description:
Object representing the required data to update an existing customer address.
    
Including address ID and any fields to be updated

address.description: The address formatted in a single string
address.example: Corrientes, 1234, 1000, Centro, Buenos Aires, Autonomous City of Buenos Aires

CustomerAddressWithError.description: Represents a customer address that failed during processing, including its input data and a detailed error message

errorMessage.description: The description of the error that occurred at trying to create or update the customer address
errorMessage.example: Customer address not found with the specified id. Both \'customerId\', \'country\', and \'street\' are required to create a new address.

salesChannelIds.description: Array of object IDs representing the different sales channels to which the customer domain will be applied

CustomerSettings.description: This setting must be used by the APP to display the habeas data agreement.
CustomerSettings.title: Customer

identifyBy.description: This setting determines if should fetch customers by email or document

HabeasData.description: The settings that will be user to accept or decline the habeas data

title.description: The title to be used in the app

link.description: The url of the file to be agreed

linkText.description: The text to be used in place of the default text to access the link
